skill.name: jutsu-creator
skill.description: Guides creation of SHINOBI WAY jutsu and combat skills, then formats balanced TypeScript skill definitions for the game.
skill.summary: Create balanced SHINOBI WAY jutsu with TypeScript-ready skill definitions.
skill.author: CesarAugustusGroB

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command-execution and weak-crypto patterns, but review found Markdown TypeScript fences, inline identifiers, and game balance terms rather than executable shell code or cryptography. No prompt injection, network access, secret access, or destructive file behavior was found in the reviewed skill files.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fences Flagged as Commands
low_findings.description: The external command detections point to Markdown TypeScript code fences and inline examples used to document game skill objects. They are not shell commands, subprocess calls, or executable automation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are fenced TypeScript documentation blocks or inline code spans. No shell interpreter, child process API, or command invocation appears in the reviewed context.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Game Terms Flagged as Crypto or Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The weak-crypto and reconnaissance detections align with normal prose and TypeScript game fields such as description, TRUE damage, stat names, and upkeep cost. No hashing, encryption, network probing, or system inventory behavior was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The scanner labels match ordinary game design text and TypeScript property values. Targeted review found no MD5, SHA1, system reconnaissance command, or network reconnaissance command.

content.value_statement: Designing new combat skills needs consistent costs, tiers, effects, and TypeScript fields. This skill guides choices and produces game-ready jutsu definitions.
actual_capabilities: Guides users through required skill fields such as name, ID, tier, action type, costs, and cooldown.
Explains elemental types, damage types, attack methods, and scaling stats for SHINOBI WAY skills.
Provides balance ranges for damage multipliers, chakra costs, cooldowns, upkeep, and tier requirements.
Supports main, toggle, side, and passive skill patterns with appropriate field guidance.
Generates TypeScript-ready skill definitions for the game constants file.
References local examples and interface documentation for consistent enum and effect usage.
limitations: It is tailored to the SHINOBI WAY game schema and may not fit other combat systems.
It does not test generated skills inside the game engine.
It relies on user-provided design goals for theme, power level, and intended role.
It does not create image assets beyond asking for an optional image path.

use_cases.title: Add a New Player Ability
use_cases.description: Create a balanced jutsu with costs, cooldowns, effects, and TypeScript fields for direct integration.

use_cases.title: Prototype Combat Content
use_cases.description: Turn a rough ability idea into a structured combat skill that follows existing game conventions.

use_cases.title: Review Skill Balance
use_cases.description: Compare a proposed skill against tier guidelines, action rules, and effect limits before implementation.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Skill
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for one simple ability.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a BASIC jutsu for SHINOBI WAY. Ask me for any missing details, then generate the TypeScript-ready skill definition.

prompt_templates.title: Design an Elemental Attack
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request with damage and element choices.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an ADVANCED elemental MAIN jutsu using lightning damage. Include cost, cooldown, scaling stat, effects, and balance reasoning.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Toggle Stance
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for ongoing combat state.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a HIDDEN TOGGLE skill with an activation cost, upkeep cost, permanent effect while active, and clear counterplay.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Forbidden Skill
prompt_templates.scenario: Expert request for balance review and revision.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this FORBIDDEN jutsu concept for balance. Adjust damage, HP cost, chakra cost, cooldown, requirements, and effects to match the guidelines.

output_examples.input: Create a basic defensive side action for an earth-focused character.
output_examples.output: A balanced BASIC side skill concept with a short shield effect, moderate chakra cost, cooldown guidance, and TypeScript-ready field choices.

output_examples.input: Make a forbidden lightning attack that feels powerful but risky.
output_examples.output: A FORBIDDEN main attack design with high chakra cost, HP sacrifice, intelligence requirement, piercing or armor-break guidance, and cooldown limits.

output_examples.input: Check whether my passive skill is too strong.
output_examples.output: A balance review comparing the passive bonuses to recommended stat ranges, slot limits, and progression requirements.

best_practices: Start with tier, action type, and combat role before selecting numbers.
Keep side actions focused on setup, defense, or utility instead of direct damage.
Use higher costs, cooldowns, or requirements when a skill has true damage or strong control effects.
anti_patterns: Do not give low-tier skills ultimate damage multipliers or rare damage properties.
Do not stack many guaranteed control effects on one low-cost skill.
Do not create toggle skills without meaningful upkeep or counterplay.

faq.question: What game is this skill designed for?
faq.answer: It is designed for SHINOBI WAY: THE INFINITE TOWER and its TypeScript skill schema.

faq.question: Can it create every field needed for a new skill?
faq.answer: Yes. It guides required fields and optional fields such as effects, requirements, passive bonuses, and images.

faq.question: Does it write files automatically?
faq.answer: No. The skill describes and generates code for the user to add to the game project.

faq.question: Can it balance existing skills?
faq.answer: Yes. It can compare a skill concept against tier, cost, cooldown, action type, and effect guidelines.

faq.question: Does it require knowledge of TypeScript?
faq.answer: Basic TypeScript familiarity helps, but the skill provides the expected enum and field structure.

faq.question: Can it make skill artwork?
faq.answer: No. It only supports an optional image path field when the user already has an asset.
